skill.name: baoyu-slide-deck
skill.description: Generate professional slide deck images from source content using structured outlines, style presets, prompt files, and sequential image generation.
skill.summary: Turn articles or pasted content into styled slide deck outlines and slide images.
skill.author: JimLiu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-severity pattern hits, but the weak-cryptography and reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by Markdown prose, style names, presentation terms, and hex color guidance. The material issue is a downstream image-generation prompt that tells the generator not to refuse sensitive or copyrighted-figure requests, plus a legitimate external slash-command workflow that writes files and calls another generator.

high_findings.title: Downstream Refusal Override in Image Prompt
high_findings.description: The base image prompt instructs the generator to create alternatives for sensitive or copyrighted figures and says not to refuse. This is not malware, but it attempts to weaken downstream safety behavior and should be revised before marketplace publication.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and appears in the prompt passed to an image-generation workflow. It is a clear safety override concern, although it is not aimed at the marketplace evaluator.

medium_findings.title: External Generator Command Invocation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to call a separate /baoyu-gemini-web command with generated prompt files and an output image path. This is legitimate for slide image generation, but it increases risk because source-derived content may be handed to another tool and files are written to the target directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is clearly documented and supports the skill purpose. Risk remains because the implementation depends on another tool whose network and data-handling behavior is not shown in this skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography locations are Markdown headings, style labels, slide outline fields, or presentation design terms. No cryptographic operation, hash use, encryption mode, or password handling was found at these cited locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain documentation and presentation style content. There is no executable cryptographic code or security-sensitive data flow.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance locations refer to slide layout whitespace, blueprint style, grid overlays, and avoiding an AI-generated writing cliche. No command for host, user, network, or environment discovery was found at these cited locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context around each cited line is presentation design guidance. The terms do not request system information or execute reconnaissance commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Model Safety Override Language
dangerous_patterns.description: The image prompt includes refusal-avoidance language for sensitive or copyrighted figures. Remove this override and let the downstream image generator apply its own safety policy.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is directly present in the downstream prompt template and could pressure a model to bypass refusal behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Tool Invocation From Skill Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow invokes /baoyu-gemini-web to generate image files from prompt files. This is expected behavior, but it should be disclosed because it may pass user content to another generation tool.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The invocation is explicit and functional, but the referenced tool behavior is outside this skill package.

content.value_statement: Long articles and notes are hard to turn into visual decks quickly. This skill creates a structured outline, style-matched slide prompts, and generated slide images.
actual_capabilities: Creates slide deck outlines from a Markdown file or pasted content.
Selects a visual style automatically from content signals or a requested preset.
Supports audience presets such as beginners, executives, experts, and general readers.
Builds per-slide prompt files for cover, content, and back-cover slides.
Generates slide images sequentially through the referenced image-generation command.
Can stop after the outline when the outline-only option is requested.
limitations: It depends on a separate image-generation command for final slide images.
Generated slides are images, not editable PowerPoint or Keynote files.
Deck length is capped at 20 slides by the skill instructions.
Image quality and text rendering depend on the downstream generator.

use_cases.title: Convert Research Into Shareable Slides
use_cases.description: Turn a report or article into a concise visual deck with sourced points and a structured narrative.

use_cases.title: Prepare Executive Briefing Images
use_cases.description: Create polished slide images for strategy summaries, investor updates, and decision briefs.

use_cases.title: Create Educational Explainers
use_cases.description: Transform tutorials or concept notes into illustrated slides for readers with a chosen audience level.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a slide deck from this content. Use the default style and generate images for each slide.

prompt_templates.title: Use a Specific Style
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a controlled visual direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a corporate slide deck from this Markdown file for executives. Keep the deck concise and visual.

prompt_templates.title: Generate an Outline First
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to review structure before image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create only the slide deck outline for this article. Use a technical style and target intermediate readers.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Complete Shareable Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 12-slide storytelling deck from this source. Preserve all key data points, write narrative headlines, and generate the slide images.

output_examples.input: A long product strategy memo with market data and recommendations.
output: A deck outline with source, style, audience, and slide count.
Slide images for the cover, body slides, and back cover.

output_examples.input: A technical tutorial about AI coding workflows.
output: A technical-style deck with schematic visual direction.
Slides that explain the workflow step by step.
A completion report listing generated image files and any failures.

best_practices: Provide source material with clear sections, facts, and intended audience.
Use outline-only mode when the narrative structure needs review before image generation.
Choose a style preset when brand tone or visual direction matters.
anti_patterns: Do not use it when you need editable presentation files with speaker notes.
Do not provide confidential source material if the downstream generator may use external services.
Do not expect the image generator to verify facts beyond the supplied source.

faq.question: Can this create a full presentation from an article?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create an outline, per-slide prompts, and slide images from a Markdown file or pasted content.

faq.question: Does it make editable PowerPoint files?
faq.answer: No. The documented output is slide images, outline files, and prompt files.

faq.question: Can I choose the visual style?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes presets such as corporate, technical, playful, minimal, storytelling, warm, retro-flat, and notion.

faq.question: Can it create only an outline?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the outline-only option when you want structure without image generation.

faq.question: How many slides can it create?
faq.answer: The skill instructions set a maximum of 20 slides.

faq.question: What security review result should users know?
faq.answer: The skill writes files and invokes another generator. Review sensitive inputs before using image generation.
